claim: Octopuses have three hearts.
note: True but surprising — a false-flag stressor.

claim: Honey does not spoil and can remain edible for thousands of years.
Wombats produce cube-shaped droppings.
A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance.
Bananas are botanically classified as berries.
The Eiffel Tower can grow more than 10 centimetres taller in summer due to thermal expansion.
Sea otters sometimes hold hands while sleeping so they do not drift apart.
Scotland's national animal is the unicorn.
Tardigrades can survive exposure to the vacuum of space.
The smell of rain hitting dry ground is called petrichor.
Octopuses have blue blood.
The pula is the official currency of Botswana.
Nintendo was originally founded in 1889 as a textile manufacturer.
note: False; it made playing cards. Plausible, not famous.

claim: The Boeing 797 entered commercial service in 2024.
Finland introduced a nationwide four-hour workday for public-sector workers in 2023.
Livermorium is the radioactive element commonly used in household smoke detectors.
Mount Kilimanjaro is the highest mountain in South Africa.
The average cumulus cloud weighs roughly 80 kilograms.
note: False; a cumulus cloud weighs hundreds of tons.

claim: Venus is the closest planet to the Sun.
The Great Barrier Reef lies off the coast of Chile.
Esperanto is an official working language of the European Union.
The novel Nineteen Eighty-Four was written by Aldous Huxley.
The human skeleton completely regenerates itself every six months.
note: False; bone turnover takes about a decade.

claim: Canberra was chosen as Australia's capital because it was the largest city in the country at the time.
note: False; it was a purpose-built compromise between Sydney and Melbourne.
